title: Japanese Shio Ramen Soup
description: A traditional Japanese ramen soup made with rich pork broth and topped with savory shio, mellow noodles, and crunchy vegetables.
instructions: 2. Place the pork bones on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly browned.
3. In a large stockpot, combine the roasted pork bones, celery, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Pour in enough water to cover the bones.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and cook for 4-5 hours, or until the broth is rich and flavorful.
5. Cook the mellow ramen noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
6. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
7. Add the shio to the broth and stir to combine.
8. Divide the cooked noodles among four bowls. Ladle the hot broth over the noodles.
9. Garnish with green onion and serve immediately.
